note: lexicon list of plants: nāgabalā, nāgalatā (betel), nāgamālikā, punnāga
thesaurus Pātālavagga: nāgaloka + nāga-king names (Ananta nāgarājā) among snake synonyms; lexical equation, no ontology
polysemy; amarakośa synonym set, nāga among sīha/kuñjara as words for 'pre-eminent/excellent one'
polysemy gloss: nāgo tu'raga(serpent) hatthīsu(elephant), nāgarukkhe(tree); entry enumerates all senses
gloss of the deva-name nāgādhipati (Virūpakkha, lord of nāgas); nāga incidental to a deva-name etymology
nāgarehi vijinitabbaṃ = to be conquered by citizens (vijita gloss)
window is a metallurgy gloss: nāga = the metal lead/tin (amarakośa 'nāgo sīsaka...'); genuine token, sense outside taxonomy
plant glosses: nāgalatā/nāgavallī (betel) 'born in the nāga-world', punnāga; nāgaloka only as folk etymology of a plant name
Pātālavagga comm; nāgakula/nāgarājā in serpent-realm river-name glosses; lexical, passing
polysemy; amarakośa: nāga (w/ sīha, byaggha, kuñjara, usabha) as compound-final word meaning 'best/pre-eminent'
lexical gloss of elephant-derived terms: nāgadantaka (ivory peg), nāgamada (elephant rut/must)
martial praise of a king (w/ sīha, kuñjara): dubbāra-nāgādi-jito, nāgādi-thāmo = elephant-strength simile
polysemy; addressed to the Buddha: 'vināyakopi nāgo si, gotamopi mahāmati' — nāga honorific of the Buddha
poetics: nāgara/upanāgara = urbane/refined (speech registers)
verse on Himavant lakes cited as a diction-flaw (pharusā) example; 'na nāgāpi ... uposathā' read for poetic ambiguity, not nāga ontology
tam-anāgamma = not coming to; sandhi false friend (na+āgamma)
jina-āgama (scripture of the Jina); sandhi false friend
polysemy; comm explicitly: when vināyaka=garuḷa then nāgo=pannaga(serpent), else Buddha — śleṣa, nāga as Buddha-epithet

claim: the Garuḷa enters the nāga-realm (nāgabhavana/nāgaloka) and seizes nāgas as prey; the nāga-kings' pride is crushed
note: poetic śleṣa, but deploys canonical nāga lore: nāga-realm + Garuḷa predation
Irandatī, a nāga-maiden (nāgamānavikā), named in a poetics example on erotic sentiment; passing
Vidhura/Puṇṇaka poetics: 'nāge ... veṭhayante' (coiling/wrapping) w/ rutting-elephant simile; serpent vs elephant unfixable
agārasmā anāgāriyaṃ pabbajati; an-āgāriya (homelessness), false friend
na+āgamesi (he did not wait); Vinaya parivāra Q&A
na+āgamesuṃ (they did not wait)

claim: a nāga in young-brahmin (māṇavaka) form ordained; once discovered, ordination of an animal (tiracchānagata) was prohibited
note: the canonical nāga-ordination locus
agārasmā anāgāriyaṃ pabbajjā; an-āgāriya false friend

claim: the arahant Majjhantika pacified/converted the wicked nāga of Kasmīra-Gandhāra
note: Saṅgīti chronicle verse; duṭṭhaṃ nāgaṃ pasādetvā
nāgā in the standard being-list (devā manussā asurā nāgā gandhabbā); Sakka's saṃyojana question; list-mention
bandhanāgārāni (prison-houses); bandhana-āgāra false friend
Dhp Nāgavagga v.157: 'eko care mātaṅgaraññeva nāgo' — wander alone like an elephant in the elephant-forest
byasana-āgame (when misfortune comes); false friend

claim: out of compassion for the nāgas the Buddha went to Nāgadīpa (to reconcile the warring nāga-kings)
note: window mixes nāgaduma/punnāgaduma (trees, scenery) with the substantive 'anukampāya nāgānaṃ nāgadīpamupāgami'
Dhammanīti; 'tiṇehi ... nāgopi bandhate' — even the elephant is bound by a grass-rope (collective strength); nāgarā=citizens also present
'muninā munināgena' — the Buddha as muni-nāga, honorific
punāguṃ = puna+āguṃ (does again); sandhi false friend
nīti: 'tiṇena vaṭṭate rajju, tāya nāgopi bandhate' — grass-rope binds even the elephant
nīti: 'sīho ... nāgamaṃsaṃ na khādati' (lion won't eat elephant-flesh) + 'nāgo gato naramukhe'; lion/elephant pairing
Rājakaṇḍa nīti: 'tiṇehi vattate yottaṃ, tena nāgopi bajjhate' — grass-rope binds the elephant
Cāṇakyanīti: byasana-āgame, dhana-āgamo; sandhi false friends
bahussutaṃ anāgamma (not approaching the learned); false friend
theranāga: 'Pālitattheranāgena' = by the elder-nāga Pālita; nāga honorific of the human thera Pālita
mettā verse: 'nāgame' = na+āgame (let it not come, of fire/anger); false friend
deva-brahma-nāga-supaṇṇa assembly honoring the Buddha (being-list); window also has nāga/punnāga trees (mixed); list-mention

claim: a nāga-king venerated Kassapa Buddha's stūpa (dwelling in an anthill); 'namo buddhāya' fell on the nāga's ear like ambrosia
note: snake-charmer (ahituṇḍika) tries to seize the nāgarāja by mantra
vimāna scenery: nāgādi-tarusaṇḍa (groves of nāga and other trees), pūga-punnāga-pāṭalī
'buddhaputtā mahānāgā' — the Buddha's sons, the great-nāgas (great monks); honorific
bandhanāgāre (prison); bandhana-āgāra false friend

claim: nāgas (with yakkhas, rakkhasas, supaṇṇas) attend a Dhamma-assembly in human guise; a nāga-king lusting after a girl loosed venom-breath to kill her but her faith protected her
note: takes_human_form + venom power
bandhanāgāre / sabbabandhanāgārāni (prisons); false friend
saccakiriyā verse: 'Nandopananda-bhogindaṃ nāgaṃ' (the nāga-king Nandopananda) among beings the Buddha subdued; name in a list
'nāgova bandhanaṃ chetvā viharāmi anāsavo' — like an elephant snapping its tether; simile of the liberated arahant
'manujāmara-nāgānaṃ brahmānañca samāgame' — beings-list (men, devas, nāgas, brahmas); window also has elephant-simile 'nāgova bandhanaṃ chetvā'; list-mention
brahma-deva-manuja-nāga-yakkha throngs viewing the buddharūpa (being-list); Mahākāla nāgarāja + nāgabhavana + nāgalatā-toothstick in tokens; list-mention

claim: a nāga of the nāgayoni took human form to ordain and was expelled; the Buddha declared nāgas 'incapable of growth (avirūḷhidhammā) in this Dhamma-Vinaya' — animal/nāga ordination barred
note: Upasampadākaṇḍa; nāgayoni, nāgabhavana, also implies magga ceiling
Vessantara white-elephant gift: mahānāga/raññanāga = the royal elephant (w/ gajuttama, kuñjara, hatthi cues); nāgarā=citizens also present
Moggallāna grammar; window is augment-rules: 'na te kānubandhanāgamesu' = nāgama (no-augment); nāginī/nāgasupaṇṇa in token list but not in window

claim: the Bodhisatta was born as the nāga-kings Bhūridatta, Campeyya, and Saṅkhapāla (practising patience as a nāga)

note: the nāgas took the Rāmagāma relics and keep them safe for the future Mahāvihāra; chronicle role (relic-guardians), no matching ontological facet
nāga-kings bring robes/ointment from nāgabhavana to the cakkavatti; nāgas incidental to a king's-glory description
hatthināgo (state-elephant) carrying the relic-casket refuses to set it down; nāgarā=citizens + Mahānāgavana placename also present
'mahānāgo' of the Buddha approaching the yakkha-assembly; mahānāgavana-uyyāna is a placename (scenery)
'nāgopi paccosakkitvā' — the war-elephant (gaja) backs up surveying the victory-ground
ākāsena+āgañchi (came through the air); sandhi false friend
golden statues of nāga-maidens (nāgakaññā/nāgamāṇavikā) around the nāgarāja in the relic chamber; artistic depiction; nāgarā=citizens also present

claim: a sāmaṇera entered the nāga-realm (nāgabhavana) and recovered relics held by the nāga-king Mahākāḷa; a deva-nāga assembly watched
note: bhikkhunāga = honorific of the monk; dominant nāga = Mahākāḷa nāgarāja

claim: the Buddha went to Nāgadīpa and reconciled the warring nāga-kings Cūḷodara and Mahodara (uncle and nephew) feuding over a gem-throne
note: Dāṭhāvaṃsa; chaddantanāgo (elephant) also in tokens

claim: the nāga-king Sumana with his nāga retinue venerated the Buddha and was established in the five precepts; the Uruvelā nāga was subdued
note: nāgamāṇavikā, nāgasampatti; hatthināga (elephant) also in tokens

claim: the nāga-king Jayasena, hearing of the parinibbāna, took a tooth-relic by nāga-iddhi power to the nāga-city (nāgapura/nāgabhavana) and enshrined it
note: nāgeddhibala (power) + realm also asserted
window is a king ordering city decoration + releasing prisoners: sakalanāgarā (all citizens), bandhanāgāre (prison); mahānāga / Nāgamahāvihāra only in token list

claim: the nāga-king Sumana, after enjoying nāga-glory (nāgasampatti), took his nāga retinue and attended the relic enshrinement
the relics were recovered from the nāga-realm (nāgabhavana/nāganagara/nāga-vimāna); the nāga-kings, unable to seize them back, lamented
note: large serpent cluster: deva-nāga-supaṇṇa, mahānāgarājā, nāgālaya
flowery proem; window nāga = the placename Mahānāgavana-uyyāna; token list mixes trees (punnāga, nāgakadamba), citizens (nāgarā), being-list (devanāgayakkha), prison (bandhanāgāre)
Jinacarita Jetavana scenery: suphulla-sāla-asana-soga-nāga + punnāga-pūga trees (pādapa); nāgarājā/phaninda (serpent) also in tokens

polysemy; window: 'Yatināgo muninā katāvakāso ... pacchimadassana' — Sāriputta (the ascetic-nāga) taking leave of the Buddha; honorific. Token list hugely mixed (kāla-nāgarājā, theranāga honorifics, trees)
Milinda questioning the thera Nāgasena about jātakas; nāga = the monk Nāgasena. Chaddanta/Sīlava nāgarāja + hatthināga (elephant) also in tokens
'aṭṭha nāgasenena desitā' — the eight taught by Nāgasena (the monk); hathināga (elephant) + nāgarājā also in window
defining akkhohiṇī (army-number): 'nava nāgasahassāni, nāge nāge sataṃ rathā' — 9000 war-elephants; caturaṅginī-senā unit
Padasādhana grammar; window: 'te e o ā kānubandhe nāgame ca na honti' = nāgama (no-augment); nāginī/nāgī only in token list
'sakkā ñātuṃ jina-āgame' (can be known in the Jina's canon); sandhi false friend

claim: the Lord of the three worlds displayed terrors to subdue the proud nāga-hosts; the nāgas dropped their weapons and venerated him
note: Samantakūṭa/Nāgadīpa episode; nāgasaṅgha, nāginda, nāgakaññā; nāgavallī/punnāga trees also in tokens
